description_long: This object is a small, lifelike bird with a streamlined body and a relatively long, straight tail. Its plumage is predominantly a muted brownish-gray, with subtle lighter and darker shading along the wings and back, and a slightly paler underside. The beak is slender, straight, and pointed, typical of many insectivorous or omnivorous urban birds. The legs are thin and appear to be a pale grayish color, tucked close to the body. The wings are folded tightly against the sides, and the tail feathers are straight and slightly separated at the tip. The overall appearance suggests a common passerine bird often found in urban parks and gardens.
description: A small, brownish-gray bird with a slender pointed beak and a straight tail, featuring subtle shading and folded wings. Its lifelike form and muted colors are typical of urban passerines.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's full profile, streamlined body, and straight tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed beak and narrow head, with legs visible beneath the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the bird's other profile and the length of the tail.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the head, body, and the straight alignment of the tail feathers.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid with subtle gradients and slight mottling on wings and back
